codeReview.summary: The change is correctly scoped to warning cleanup. In `crates/agents-openai/src/provider.rs`, the two default-URL helpers were removed from the production import list and re-imported only inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`, while the provider's runtime methods and transport-selection logic remained unchanged. This satisfies the feature goal of eliminating the unused-import warning without changing provider behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `runtime-parity-worker` skill is written for behavior/parity work and assumes upstream reference reads plus failing-test-first flow. This warning-only cleanup was correctly completed without those steps, so the skill could explicitly allow narrow no-behavior hygiene fixes as an exception instead of implying test-first/reference work is always required.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature description in `features.json` scopes work to removing an unused-import warning without behavior changes, and the transcript skeleton for worker session `456d9099-0729-41b3-aa9c-2e03649585fc` shows no `reference/` reads or failing-test-first step before the edit to `crates/agents-openai/src/provider.rs`.

summary: Pass. The commit is a minimal, non-behavioral provider cleanup that removes the stale top-level imports and keeps the default helper imports only where the tests use them; no correctness issues were found in the reviewed diff.
